Package org.eventb.internal.core.ast.datatype

Class TypeConstructorExtension

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.ast.datatype.TypeConstructorExtension
All Implemented Interfaces:
ITypeConstructorExtension, IExpressionExtension, IFormulaExtension
public class TypeConstructorExtension extends Object implements ITypeConstructorExtension
This class represents an extension used as type constructor for a datatype.

The type constructor type represents the datatype type and its children corresponds to the datatype type parameters in the verifyType(Type, Expression[], Predicate[]), synthesizeType(Expression[], Predicate[], ITypeMediator) and { typeCheck(ExtendedExpression, ITypeCheckMediator) methods.

This class must not override equals as this would wreak havoc in formula factories. We rely on object identity for identifying identical type constructors.

  • Method Details

    • conjoinChildrenWD

      public boolean conjoinChildrenWD()
      Description copied from interface: IFormulaExtension
      Whether or not children WD is conjoined to the returned WD predicate.

      If true, the resulting WD is 'getWDPredicate() and WD(children)'. If false, the resulting WD is just 'getWDPredicate()'.

      In most cases, children WD shall be conjoined. Reasons not to do so include the case where a WD of the form 'P and ( P => WD(children) )' is desired. In the latter case, it is the responsibility of IFormulaExtension.getWDPredicate(IExtendedFormula, IWDMediator) to explicitly embed the WD conditions for children in the returned predicate.

      Specified by:
      conjoinChildrenWD in interface IFormulaExtension
      Returns:
      true iff children WD is conjoined.

      public Predicate getWDPredicate(IExtendedFormula formula, IWDMediator wdMediator)

    • typeCheck

      public Type typeCheck(ExtendedExpression expression, ITypeCheckMediator tcMediator)
      Description copied from interface: IExpressionExtension
      Define type check constraints for the given extended expression. The given type check mediator is intended to be used for creating type variables when needed, and for adding type constraints.
      Specified by:
      typeCheck in interface IExpressionExtension
      Parameters:
      expression - the extended expression to type check
      tcMediator - a type check mediator
      Returns:
      the type of the given extended expression (in terms of type variables if needed)

    • synthesizeType

      public Type synthesizeType(Expression[] childExprs, Predicate[] childPreds, ITypeMediator mediator)
      Description copied from interface: IExpressionExtension
      Computes the type of the extended expression from its given children.
      Specified by:
      synthesizeType in interface IExpressionExtension
      Parameters:
      childExprs - the child expressions
      childPreds - the child predicates
      Returns:
      the type of the extended expression or null if it is ill-typed or if its type cannot be determined

    • verifyType

      public boolean verifyType(Type proposedType, Expression[] childExprs, Predicate[] childPreds)
      Description copied from interface: IExpressionExtension
      Verifies that the given proposed type is coherent with the given children.
      Specified by:
      verifyType in interface IExpressionExtension
      Parameters:
      proposedType - a type proposed for the extended expression
      childExprs - the child expressions
      childPreds - the child predicates
      Returns:
      true iff the proposed type is coherent with the children, false otherwise

    • isATypeConstructor

      public boolean isATypeConstructor()
      Description copied from interface: IExpressionExtension

      Most commonly, this method returns false. Returning true is reserved FOR ADVANCED PURPOSES ONLY.

      Can be true only if this extension is a type constructor, which implies, as a contract for maintaining inner coherence, that the IExpressionExtension.synthesizeType(Expression[], Predicate[], ITypeMediator) method returns a powerset of the parametric type instantiated with this extension and the base type of its children if any.

      Moreover, a type constructor must not take any predicate child. This restriction will be enforced when creating a parametric type with this extension.

      Specified by:
      isATypeConstructor in interface IExpressionExtension
      Returns:
      true iff this extension is a type constructor

    • getOrigin

      public Datatype getOrigin()
      Description copied from interface: ITypeConstructorExtension
      Returns the datatype to which this type constructor extension belongs.
      Specified by:
      getOrigin in interface IFormulaExtension
      Specified by:
      getOrigin in interface ITypeConstructorExtension
      Returns:
      the datatype of this type constructor extension

    • getFormalNames

      public String[] getFormalNames()
      Description copied from interface: ITypeConstructorExtension
      Returns the name of the formal type parameters used to define the datatype. These names are purely decorative but form a good basis for defining variables after them.
      Specified by:
      getFormalNames in interface ITypeConstructorExtension
      Returns:
      the names of the formal type parameters
